claim: A cheaper governed agent layer can replace this stack without slowing launches.
observedState: Hidden approval dependencies still require manual exception handling during agent launches.
notes: Savings case exists but still depends on approval-rail cleanup.

claim: Replacement is possible this year with low switching risk.
observedState: Manual access-review exception handling still blocks a clean exit story.
notes: Replacement case is compelling, but the exception map is incomplete.

claim: Overlap makes this a fast savings win.
observedState: Overlap is real, but duplicate logic and exception mappings still make the switch more heroic than the board brief suggests.
notes: A good candidate once attribution proof is reissued.

claim: This is mostly overlap tax now.
observedState: Duplicate journeys and overlapping audience sync still burn spend with limited strategic upside.
notes: Clearest replacement candidate in the current estate.

claim: Warehouse spend can drop quickly with a different platform mix.
observedState: Backfill and lineage dependencies are still hidden enough that the savings case is ahead of reality.
notes: Savings narrative needs a more honest migration pack.

claim: A simpler workflow tool can absorb this use case.
observedState: Manual exception handling still touches revenue-close operations, so the board story is ahead of the process reality.
notes: Possible win, but not before close-process hygiene improves.

claim: This tool can be retired with low organizational pain.
observedState: Usage is shallow and overlap with existing suites is already obvious.
notes: Low drama and easy to explain.

claim: A consolidation move will lower spend and simplify ops.
observedState: The value is real, but several hidden dependencies and manual exceptions still complicate a clean switch.
notes: Needs a much cleaner dependency map before the board should bank the savings.
